title: Olusatrum
intro: Olusatrum, also known as sea kale, is a biennial plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is native to coastal areas of Europe and North Africa. The plant is rich in nutrients and has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Olusatrum include isothiocyanates, glucosinolates, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Olusatrum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
